chapter_title.ar: باب جَامِعٌ فِي الدَّوَابِّ الَّتِي لَا تُؤْكَلُ لَحْمُهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 2 – The comprehensive regarding the animals whose flesh cannot be consumed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/32)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/32)
text: كُلُّ ذِي نَابٍ مِنَ السِّبَاعِ وَ مِخْلَبٍ مِنَ الطَّيْرِ حَرَامٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Mahboub, from Dawood Bin Farqad,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘All the ones with fangs (pointed teeth) from the predatory animals, and claws from the birds, are Prohibited’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn Mahbub from Dawud ibn Farqad who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ’Of the beasts that which have canine teeth and of birds those which have claws are not lawful for food.’”
